schools.summary: Gill: the Lord spoke out of the tabernacle, having before directed the dividing of the land, and now speaks again.
Henry: many things ordered by the law of Moses to be done when they came to Canaan, now to be performed.

themes: The LORD also spake unto Joshua, saying
God mindful of mercy, providing a refuge before the need
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble (Ps. 46:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the affair concerned the children of Israel, now having the whole land divided.
Keil & Delitzsch: the command to appoint cities of refuge whereof God spake by Moses.

themes: Appoint out for you cities of refuge, whereof I spake unto you by the hand of Moses
God's mercy purposed of old and now performed, a shadow of Christ
fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us (Heb. 6:18)

schools.summary: Gill: one who through mere accident, without design or malice, killed a person, friend or foe, kindred or stranger.
Wesley: 'unwittingly' means through ignorance, error, or mistake.

themes: the slayer that killeth any person unawares and unwittingly may flee thither... your refuge from the avenger of blood
the fleeing slayer a figure of the sinner fleeing to Christ
we... have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope (Heb. 6:18)

schools.summary: Gill: he flees to the city nearest him.
Wesley: the gate, where the judges sat; he gives a true relation of the fact, and if satisfied they take him in.

themes: he... shall stand at the entering of the gate... and declare his cause... they shall take him into the city... that he may dwell among them
Christ receiving all who come to him owning their need
him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out (John 6:37)

schools.summary: Gill: the avenger of blood might pursue him to the city and demand him; the slayer hated him not beforetime, and smote him unwittingly.

themes: they shall not deliver the slayer up into his hand; because he smote his neighbour unwittingly
the perfect safety of those sheltered in God's refuge
there is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus (Rom. 8:1)

schools.summary: Gill: he dwelt there until his cause was heard, and if found guilty only of accidental manslaughter, was kept safe until the high priest's death, then might return to his own city.

themes: he shall dwell in that city... until the death of the high priest... then shall the slayer return... unto his own city
the believer freed and restored by the death of the great High Priest
by means of death... they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance (Heb. 9:15)

schools.summary: Gill: the appointment of these three cities was made by the children of Israel at this time.
Keil & Delitzsch: Kedesh in Galilee, in the north, among them.

themes: they appointed Kedesh in Galilee... and Shechem in mount Ephraim, and Kirjatharba, which is Hebron
the refuge made accessible to all, its names figures of Christ
he be not far from every one of us (Acts 17:27)

schools.summary: Gill: these were in the country possessed by Reuben, Gad, and the half tribe of Manasseh.
Keil & Delitzsch: Bezer, Ramoth in Gilead, and Golan in Bashan.

themes: on the other side Jordan... they assigned Bezer... and Ramoth in Gilead... and Golan in Bashan
the refuge provided for all God's people on every side
the name of the LORD is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe (Prov. 18:10)

schools.summary: Gill: they were for the common use of all, not for that tribe only in which they stood.
Keil & Delitzsch: for all the children of Israel and for the stranger that sojourned among them.

themes: These were the cities appointed for all the children of Israel, and for the stranger that sojourneth among them
the refuge open to Jew and Gentile alike, the whosoever of the gospel
the same Lord over all is rich unto all that call upon him (Rom. 10:12)
